title: U.S. Plan Is Said to Pull a Third of Fighter Jets It Provides NATO for Europe
summary: The plan, outlined by officials and in a written document, provides rare clarity about the extent to which the Trump administration intends to reduce its commitment to NATO.

summary: Plus, the Friday news quiz.

title: How SpaceX’s IPO Compares With Saudi Aramco, Uber and Others
summary: The previous record was set over six years ago. But SpaceX, whose stock begins trading on Friday, is on track to raise tens of billions of dollars more.

title: SpaceX IPO: How Our Reporters Assess the Sky-High Valuation and Potential Economic Impact
summary: Two Times tech reporters and their editor talk about assessing the sky-high valuation of the rocket and satellite maker, the potential economic impact of its public debut and the role of the world’s richest man in it all.

title: A Dangerous Limbo Leaves Iran, and the World, Between Peace and War
summary: Since announcing a nominal cease-fire two months ago, Iran, Israel and the U.S. have remained locked in low-intensity violence that has become a new normal.

title: The World Is Draining Oil Reserves, Raising Pressure for a Peace Deal
summary: The amount of oil and fuel stored by businesses and governments has fallen sharply since the start of the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.

title: Risk Strait of Hormuz or Wait? Shipping Companies Face a Costly Dilemma.
summary: With more than 500 ships still stranded in the Persian Gulf, pressure on the shipowners and sailors is growing by the day.

title: Kennedy Center Appeals Order to Remove Trump’s Name
summary: One day before a deadline to take the president’s name off its facade, the arts institution appealed a federal judge’s ruling that also temporarily blocked it from closing.

title: Some States Opt Out of Trump’s ‘Great American State Fair’
summary: At least five have declined to participate, the latest sign that the national 250th birthday celebration has become a fragmented and partisan affair as the president seeks his imprint.

title: Trump Suspends Funding for Los Angeles Homeless Agency
summary: The Trump administration cited misspending among the reasons for blocking funds to the Los Angeles Homeless Services Authority. Mayor Karen Bass warned that “people will lose their lives.”

title: Inside Cesar Chavez’s Labor Union, Women Faced a Culture of Sexual Abuse
summary: Many of the women who helped lead the fight for Latino civil rights say they were quietly waging their own battles with harassment, misogyny and sexual assault inside the powerful United Farm Workers union.

title: David Hockney, Who Restored the Human Form to Art, Dies at 88
summary: His colorful figurative paintings were both conservative and iconoclastic, defying the dominant abstract schools of the mid-20th century.

title: What I Learned From David Hockney
summary: The curator Norman Rosenthal knew the artist for over 60 years and still discovered something new when they collaborated on a final blockbuster show.

title: With iPhones and Faxes, David Hockney Embraced Tech
summary: Polaroids and photocopiers also gave the artist possibilities for creating in forms vastly different from his paintings.

title: After Senate Loss, Cornyn Predicts ‘Miserable’ Final Two Years for Trump
summary: In his first extensive interview since his defeat by a Trump-backed challenger, the Texas Republican said the Senate was in for a “bumpy ride” as he and others flex new political freedom.

title: Frisco Mayor’s Race Tests Anti-Muslim Politics in Texas
summary: A runoff in the Dallas suburb of Frisco is testing whether anti-Muslim rhetoric, prominent in G.O.P. primaries this year, can win over a broader set of voters.

title: Excitement and Frustration Mix as the World Cup Comes to America
summary: Astronomical ticket prices, soaring security costs and concern over traffic and transit snarls is mixed with pride in host cities and excitement over the U.S. team.

title: AIPAC Wants Democrats to Back Israel. Instead, They’re Turning on AIPAC.

title: Major League Baseball Will Question the Dodgers’s Doctor About Banned Drugs
summary: The inquiry comes after a report that Dr. Neal ElAttrache, the physician for the team, supported the U.F.C. star Conor McGregor in using performance-enhancing drugs while recovering from an injury.

title: Sheriffs in Maryland Challenge State Limits on Cooperation With ICE
summary: A lawsuit by a group of 17 county law enforcement officers is another front in the Trump-era fight over local police’s role in immigration enforcement.

summary: Competitions that give soldiers a brief break from the front have a festival-like atmosphere, a mix of potentially deadly weapons and frolicking children.

title: Knicks in Five? Please Don’t Tempt the Jinx Gods.

summary: The SpaceX I.P.O. will enable its founder to build — and scale — his peculiar vision of society.

title: Anti-Immigrant Riots after Belfast Stabbing Leave City on Edge
